_meta.scope: Unmodeled abilities present in curated shipped teams or the Champions mega registry.

Adaptability.why: 1.5x STAB materially shifts Basculegion and Mega Lucario/Glimmora damage ranges.

Aerilate.why: Type conversion plus damage boost changes Mega Pinsir damage and STAB coverage.

Armor Tail.why: Blocks opposing priority into the side and changes Fake Out/Sucker Punch lines.

Berserk.why: Special Attack boost on HP threshold changes Mega Drampa snowball turns.

Blaze.why: Low-HP Fire boost changes Charizard damage in close endgames.

Bulletproof.why: Projectile immunities are real but Mega Chesnaught is not in current shipped teams.

Clear Body.why: Blocks Intimidate and stat drops on Dragapult, changing lead pressure and damage.

Cloud Nine.why: Suppresses weather effects, changing speed tiers and sun/rain damage math.

Competitive.why: Turns Intimidate and other drops into Milotic Special Attack boosts.

Compound Eyes.why: Accuracy boost changes hit rates for status and utility lines.

Defiant.why: Turns Intimidate and stat drops into Kingambit Attack boosts.

Earth Eater.why: Ground immunity plus healing changes partner Earthquake interactions.

Fairy Aura.why: Field-wide Fairy damage boost changes Mega Floette and partner damage.

Filter.why: Super-effective damage reduction matters, but only on mega-only catalog rows today.

Flower Veil.why: Protects allied Grass types from stat drops and status in supported lines.

Friend Guard.why: Passive ally damage reduction changes KO thresholds in Maushold boards.

Frisk.why: Item reveal is already effectively visible in the sim and does not alter battle math.

Gale Wings.why: Priority Flying moves change Talonflame turn order and lead pressure.

Good as Gold.why: Status immunity changes Taunt, Encore, Thunder Wave, and utility targeting.

Healer.why: End-turn ally status cure can matter, but it is mega-only and relatively narrow.

Huge Power.why: Attack doubling is a major damage multiplier on Mega Starmie.

Infiltrator.why: Bypasses Substitute and screens, changing damage and status delivery.

Innards Out.why: Punishes KOs with reflected HP loss and can swing trades.

Insomnia.why: Sleep immunity matters on Ariados, but it is narrower than the top coverage gaps.

Iron Fist.why: Punching move boost changes Mega Golurk and Mega Crabominable damage.

Limber.why: Paralysis immunity matters, but current exposure is one Mega Lopunny team.

Magic Bounce.why: Status reflection changes matchup flow against Taunt, sleep, and hazards-style utility.

Mega Launcher.why: Pulse move boost materially changes Mega Blastoise damage.

Mind's Eye.why: Ghost immunity bypass and accuracy behavior affect Ursaluna-Bloodmoon lines.

Mold Breaker.why: Ignores defensive abilities and changes interaction outcomes for multiple Megas.

Mummy.why: Ability overwrite on contact is real, but it is less common than the top-tier gaps.

No Guard.why: Perfect accuracy changes both move reliability and opposing hit rates.

Overgrow.why: Low-HP Grass boost changes Meganium damage in close games.

Pixilate.why: Type conversion plus damage boost changes Sylveon and Mega Altaria outputs.

Poison Touch.why: Contact poison chance changes Sneasler chip and status pressure.

Prankster.why: Priority status moves materially change Whimsicott, Sableye, and Liepard turn order.

Pressure.why: PP drain is not modeled, so Pressure has no active mechanical path today.

Protean.why: Type-changing on move use radically changes STAB and defensive matchups.

Pure Power.why: Attack doubling is a major damage multiplier on Mega Medicham.

Refrigerate.why: Type conversion plus damage boost changes Mega Glalie damage and coverage.

Regenerator.why: Switch healing changes Amoonguss cycling and board longevity.

Rough Skin.why: Contact chip affects many Garchomp trades and KO ranges.

Sand Force.why: Sand-boosted Rock, Ground, and Steel damage matters on Mega Steelix/Garchomp.

Sand Veil.why: Weather evasion can matter, but it is narrower and RNG-heavier than core gaps.

Scrappy.why: Ghost immunity bypass changes Fake Out and Fighting/Normal targeting.

Shadow Tag.why: Trapping fundamentally changes switch options and perish-style endgames.

Sheer Force.why: Damage boost plus secondary-effect suppression changes Mega Camerupt outputs.

Shell Armor.why: Crit immunity matters, but it is mega-only and less frequent than the top gaps.

Skill Link.why: Guaranteeing multihit move counts changes Mega Heracross damage dramatically.

Snow Cloak.why: Weather evasion can matter, but it is narrower and RNG-heavier than core gaps.

Solar Power.why: Sun damage boost and recoil change Mega Houndoom and Charizard-Y math.

Stalwart.why: Ignoring redirection changes doubles targeting for Mega Skarmory.

Stamina.why: Defense boosts on hit change Archaludon survivability over multi-hit turns.

Stance Change.why: Aegislash form swaps alter stats, damage, and survivability every turn.

Strong Jaw.why: Bite move boost changes Mega Sharpedo damage.

Sturdy.why: Surviving from full HP changes KO math and turn sequencing.

Supreme Overlord.why: Late-game damage scaling changes Kingambit closing power.

Technician.why: Low-base-power move boost changes Mega Scizor damage benchmarks.

Thick Fat.why: Fire and Ice damage reduction changes Mega Venusaur survival ranges.

Tough Claws.why: Contact damage boost changes Charizard-X and Aerodactyl-Mega outputs.

Trace.why: Copied-ability edge cases can matter, but all current exposure is mega-only.

Unaware.why: Ignoring opposing stat boosts changes setup matchups and endgame reads.

Unnerve.why: Berry suppression matters, but it is narrower than the current top mechanic gaps.
